Fit Partially Linear DML via DoubleML (R) when available

Source: R/DMLearner.R
doubleml_plr.Rd

If the DoubleML package is installed, fits a partially linear regression model using DoubleML::DoubleMLPLR and returns ATE and optional CATE predictions. Otherwise returns NULL. Useful for comparison with LinearDML or for using DoubleML's inference (e.g. confint, bootstrap). Requires mlr3 and mlr3learners (dependencies of DoubleML). For a pre-built DoubleMLData object and arbitrary learners (including pipelines), use doubleml_plr_fit_data.

Usage

doubleml_plr(
  X,
  treatment,
  y,
  ml_l = NULL,
  ml_m = NULL,
  ml_g = NULL,
  n_folds = 5L,
  n_rep = 1L,
  score = "partialling out",
  apply_cross_fitting = TRUE,
  dml_procedure = "dml2",
  return_ml_object = FALSE
)

Arguments

X

covariate matrix or data.frame

treatment

treatment vector (binary 0/1 uses classif learner by default; continuous \(D\) uses regr)

y

outcome vector

ml_l

mlr3 learner for \(E[Y|X]\) (default: lrn("regr.ranger"))

ml_m

mlr3 learner for \(E[D|X]\) (default: classif.ranger if treatment is binary, else regr.ranger)

ml_g

mlr3 learner for \(g_0(X) = E[Y - D\theta_0|X]\); required for score = "IV-type" or a custom score function (default: clone of ml_l when needed)

n_folds, n_rep

cross-fitting folds and sample-splitting repetitions (passed to DoubleMLPLR)

score

"partialling out", "IV-type", or a custom score function(y, d, l_hat, m_hat, g_hat, smpls) returning list(psi_a, psi_b)

apply_cross_fitting

logical; if FALSE, no cross-fitting (see DoubleML docs)

dml_procedure

"dml1" or "dml2"

return_ml_object

if TRUE, return the fitted DoubleMLPLR object as dml_obj

Value

List with ate, ate_se, cate (if predict available), and optionally dml_obj. Or NULL if DoubleML not installed.

References

DoubleML: https://docs.doubleml.org ; Bach et al. (2021) arXiv:2103.09603

See also

RCausalML-package

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# doubleml_plr(...)
} # }
